evals.prompt: Create a new skill that helps debug SPINE microservices by checking Kafka consumer lag and recent error logs via dtctl
evals.expected_output: A skill directory with SKILL.md, no external URLs, safe scripts using subprocess list form, Dynatrace-aware instructions for dtctl usage
expectations: The generated skill has a valid SKILL.md with name and description in frontmatter
No external URLs appear in any generated files
Any Python scripts use subprocess with list args, not os.system or shell=True
The skill references dtctl or Dynatrace-specific tools
The safety validator passes on the generated skill

evals.prompt: I have an existing skill at ~/.claude/skills/my-old-skill that uses os.system() and fetches from an external CDN. Help me make it safe for our Dynatrace environment.
evals.expected_output: Identification of safety issues, concrete fixes replacing os.system with subprocess, removing CDN references, and re-validation
expectations: The skill identifies os.system usage as a safety issue
The skill identifies external CDN URLs as a safety issue
Concrete replacement code is provided using subprocess with list args
The safety validator is run before and after changes
No new external URLs are introduced in the fix

evals.prompt: Run the description optimizer on my skill at ~/.claude/skills/service-health-check to improve its triggering accuracy
evals.expected_output: Generation of trigger eval queries, running the optimization loop, and applying the best description
expectations: A set of should-trigger and should-not-trigger eval queries is generated
The optimization loop script is invoked with appropriate parameters
The best-performing description is presented to the user
Before/after comparison of the description is shown
